entry.title: Re: sap btp trail account integration suite login error
entry.content:
Hi @naveennasaka ,

This usually happens when the Integration Suite session remains idle for a long time and you attempt to refresh the page.

This is a common issue. You can resolve it by reopening the Integration Suite either from the BTP Cockpit or by using the actual Integration Suite login URL (if you have it bookmarked or saved in a notepad) and pasting it again into the browser. This should redirect you back to the Integration Suite page successfully.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: Migration fixed asset nbv less than 1
entry.content:
Hi,

the easiest way in this case is to set the indicator "Negative Values allowed" to "Yes" in the depreciation area settings of the asset master record.

As you use "memo values" your takeover data is not consistent and violates the configuration settings. Thus the error message is justified. But setting above indicator your "overrule" this setting specifically for this asset in its valuation settings.

Best Regards,
Markus

entry.title: SAP Business Network: How to choose the right account or subscription for your business
entry.content:
A few days ago we brought suppliers together to address a question that comes up time and again — which account or subscription actually fits your business? Here is what we covered.

Whether you are new to SAP Business Network or already transacting, you may recognise and even relate to some of these questions:

  • What type of account or subscription do I need?
  • What are the limitations of a Standard vs Enterprise account?
  • How much do subscriptions cost?
  • When should I upgrade?
  • How do I decide if I am not sure how much business I will get from my customers on the network?

We aim to bring clarity to exactly these questions.

What are the options? 

Suppliers have 3 options when it comes down to it: 

  1. Standard account - free account. 
  2. Enterprise account – consists of various subscription tiers and transaction thresholds.
  3. Promote subscription:  a value-added add on subscription to boost sales via higher search ranking, network catalogs and access to SAP Business Network Discovery leads and RFx opportunities.

How do you decide?

The decision process should be based on these factors:

  1. Your number of customers along with current or projected transaction volumes
  2. Your business goals - as part of a growth strategy and new customer acquisition.
  3. Your operational needs - system integration, analytics, compliance etc.

As a rule of thumb, Standard account is a good start for low-volumes or single-buyer scenarios. 

On the other hand, suppliers with higher-volume or those seeking integration, dedicated support and data insights will get more value from an Enterprise account.

Is it worth it?

Independent IDC research reports a 416% three-year ROI for suppliers on the network, with benefits including revenue growth, stronger governance and compliance, and improved cross-sell/up-sell effectiveness.

Next Steps:

  • Explore account and subscription options on our Community for Suppliers page
  • Watch the replay this 30-min session led by our supplier-dedicated NEAT team and don’t miss the open Q&A part at the end for more insights shared with fellow suppliers. 
  • Join the next webinar on how to 'Unlock the Full Power of SAP Business Network: A Deep Dive into Enterprise Account Benefits' June 10th at 11 AM CEST, 11am EST , 12.30 AEDT or in other languages here.
  • Already have an Enterprise account and want to talk through specific challenges?  Feel free to contact our NEAT team for a free consultation.

In conclusion, no one size fits all and that is exactly the point.  These options exist to better suit suppliers and meet them where they are at, whatever their challenges, setup or ambitions. 

What factors matter most to you when choosing the right setup on SAP Business Network — and have you found the right fit yet? Share your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: Re: How to import a Groovy class reference in Resources->...
entry.content:
Hi @Integration12345 ,

Great question! When you move a POGO class from a Local Script to a Common Script Collection, the import path changes because the classpath namespace shifts.

When the class is in a Local Script, the import works as:
import src.main.resources.script.ClassName

However, when moved to a Common Script Collection, the classpath includes the Script Collection's Artifact ID. So the import path becomes:
import src.main.resources.script.<ScriptCollectionArtifactID>.ClassName

For example, if your Script Collection Artifact ID is 'CommonScripts':
import src.main.resources.script.CommonScripts.ClassName

A few things to double-check:

1. Artifact ID vs Display Name - Make sure you're using the Artifact ID of the Script Collection (set during creation), not its display name. You can verify this in your CPI tenant under the Script Collection details.

2. File and Class Name Match - The Groovy file name must exactly match the class name (e.g., ClassName.groovy containing class ClassName).

3. Add Script Collection as Dependency - In your iFlow, go to References → Script Collection and ensure the Common Script Collection is listed as a dependency. Without this, the import will fail even with the correct path.

4. Redeploy Both Artifacts - After making changes, redeploy the Script Collection first, then the iFlow. CPI caches scripts aggressively, so a redeploy of just the iFlow may not be enough.

Hope this helps!

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: Transaction Type (RMVCT) Missing in ACDOCA for TRM &...
entry.content:
Hi Ankita,

For RE-FX I don't know, but for TRM you can use the following customizing to populate the transaction type according to your rules:

IMG -> Treasury and Risk Management -> Transaction Manager -> General Settings -> Accounting -> Link to Other Accounting Components -> Additional Account Assignments -> Define Derivation of Additional Account Assignments

Create a rule for target field: TARGETS RMVCT

Alternatively, you could also implement BAdI TPM_ACCIF_TRAC, which is called in TPM10 (but standard solution above should be preferred).

Best regards
Felix

entry.title: Auto-Populating Transfer of Control Dates for 5MQ Intercompany Sales — Billing Date as the Source
entry.content:
Background

In a previous blog post, one scenario of auto-populating the External Actual Transfer of Control Date was discussed. Today we continue with another variation — this time in the context of 5MQ (Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit), where the date source and trigger are different.

The business requirement here has two parts:

  1. When an outbound delivery is created, default the External Planned Transfer of Control Date to 9999-12-31 — this keeps the POD window open indefinitely and prevents it from expiring before billing takes place.
  2. When the delivery is saved after billing, automatically populate the External Actual Transfer of Control Date from the corresponding Billing Document Date (posting date of the billing document).

Both fields are sap:updatable="false" in the standard OData API (API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV), so BAdI-based extensibility is the only viable path.

The two fields in question on the outbound delivery Shipment tab:

  • External Planned Transfer of Control Date (INTCOEXTPLNDTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME)
  • External Actual Transfer of Control Date (INTCOEXTACTLTRANSFOFCTRLDTETME)

Enhancement Points

BAdI 1 — LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD (Key User Extensibility)

Purpose: Default the Planned Transfer of Control Date to 9999-12-31 at delivery creation.

Entry point: Custom Logic app (#CustomLogic-develop) — Key User Extensibility, no transport required.

Implementation:

 
delivery_document_out = delivery_document_in.

DATA lv_max_ts TYPE timestamp.
lv_max_ts = '99991231000000'.

IF delivery_document_in-intcoextplndtransfofctrldtetme IS INITIAL.
  delivery_document_out-intcoextplndtransfofctrldtetme = lv_max_ts.
ENDIF.

This BAdI operates under a FUNCTIONAL transactional contract — no database reads allowed, which is fine here since we only need a field assignment.

BAdI 2 — LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE (Key User Extensibility)

Purpose: Populate the Actual Transfer of Control Date from the billing document date when the delivery is saved.

Entry point: Custom Logic app (#CustomLogic-develop) — same as above.

Why not LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD for this? LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEAD enforces a FUNCTIONAL contract at runtime — any SELECT statement raises a transactional contract violation error. LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE does not have this restriction and allows reads against standard CDS views.

Implementation:

 
delivery_document_out = delivery_document_in.

DATA lv_max_ts TYPE timestamp.
lv_max_ts = '99991231000000'.
delivery_document_out-intcoextplndtransfofctrldtetme = lv_max_ts.

CHECK delivery_document_in-shippingpoint = '1311' OR
      delivery_document_in-shippingpoint = '1320'.

CHECK delivery_document_in-intcoextactltransfofctrldtetme IS INITIAL.

SELECT SINGLE t~billingdocumentdate
  FROM i_billingdocument AS t
    INNER JOIN i_billingdocumentitem AS i
      ON i~billingdocument = t~billingdocument
  WHERE i~referencesddocument         = @delivery_document_in-deliverydocument
    AND i~referencesddocumentcategory = 'J'
    AND t~billingdocumentiscancelled  = ''
  INTO @DATA(lv_billing_date).

IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_billing_date IS NOT INITIAL.
  DATA(lv_date_str) = |{ lv_billing_date DATE = RAW }|.
  DATA lv_timestamp TYPE timestamp.
  lv_timestamp = |{ lv_date_str }000000|.
  delivery_document_out-intcoextactltransfofctrldtetme = lv_timestamp.
ENDIF.

Design notes:

  • Scoped to specific Shipping Points — adjust shippingpoint values to match your configuration
  • CHECK IS INITIAL on the Actual date ensures idempotency — the date is only set once and won't be overwritten on subsequent saves
  • ReferenceSDDocumentCategory = 'J' filters for outbound deliveries in the document flow
  • Date-to-timestamp conversion uses string template syntax; CONVERT DATE ... INTO TIME STAMP is not available in the Custom Logic environment
  • No custom tables or Z CDS views required — standard CDS views I_BillingDocument and I_BillingDocumentItem are sufficient

Test Result

Outbound delivery 80000325, Shipping Point 1311/1320, with an existing billing document dated 05/28/2026:

before

after 

The Actual Transfer of Control Date matches the billing document date. The Planned date remains 9999-12-31 as expected.

One thing need to mention, after billing is completed and the invoice has a posting date, it is still necessary to manually trigger an outbound delivery update in order for the update to be successful.

Summary

Requirement BAdI Contract Approach

Default Planned date to 9999-12-31LE_SHP_MODIFY_HEADFUNCTIONALDirect field assignment
Populate Actual date from billingLE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARENo restrictionSELECT on I_BillingDocument + I_BillingDocumentItem

Both implementations are maintained entirely in the Custom Logic Fiori app — no developer extensibility, no transport requests.

The features covered in this article are based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2602, please refer to the latest information for changes in subsequent versions.

Hope you LIKE it if it addresses your issue. After that, please feel free to comment after following my account and I will reply ASAP.

entry.title: Re: Read Multiple Attachments in CPI
entry.content:
Hi @mashfaq ,

The issue is happening because the Mail adapter is still processing the complete attachment map instead of a single attachment during each loop iteration.

From your properties:

AttachmentCount = 2
AttachmentList = {39AHD.pdf=..., 0395_20260518135718.102_X.pdf=...}

the loop is iterating correctly, but the attachment handling inside the loop is not removing or isolating one attachment at a time.

To send exactly one attachment per email, you need to:

  1. Read one attachment from the AttachmentList

  2. Set only that attachment to the message

  3. Remove previous attachments before the next iteration

Usually this is handled with a Groovy script inside the Looping Process Call.

Example approach:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import javax.activation.DataHandler

def Message processData(Message message) {

    def attachments = message.getAttachments()

    // Clear all existing attachments first
    message.getAttachmentWrapperObjects().clear()

    // Pick single attachment
    def firstKey = attachments.keySet().iterator().next()

    DataHandler dh = attachments.get(firstKey)

    // Add only one attachment
    message.addAttachmentObject(firstKey, dh)

    return message
}

Also verify:

  • The Looping Process Call is decrementing AttachmentCount

  • The processed attachment is removed from AttachmentList

  • The Mail adapter is not configured to automatically include all message attachments

Most importantly, after each iteration you should remove the processed attachment from the map; otherwise CPI keeps reusing the full attachment collection in every loop.

That is why you currently receive either:

  • all attachments in every email, or

  • empty emails after attachments are cleared.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: France E-Invoicing Set up from S4H public Cloud Via S...
entry.content:
Hi @Jyothi5 ,

This looks more like a system limitation/current SAP-supported setup rather than a configuration issue from your side.

At the moment, SAP supports the France E-Invoicing scenario only through the cf-eu02 BTP region. So, if your subaccount is in cf-eu10, the France DRC process may not work there currently.

The note about the “qualification system” simply means that SAP currently allows customers to test the integration with the French tax authority’s test environment only. The actual production/live connection is not yet fully available.

So in simple terms:

  • cf-eu02 is currently the required/supported region for this scenario.
  • Only testing connectivity is supported for now.
  • This appears to be an SAP platform restriction/current rollout status, not an issue with your implementation.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Unlocking Fiori Applications for Process Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
When working with Fiori applications related to process orders — such as Create Process Order (C0R1), Change Process Order (C0R2), Display Process Order (C0R3), and Confirm Process Order Phase (C0R6N) — you may notice that these apps and their associated business catalogs are not readily available in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This can be puzzling, especially when scope items like 42N, 3UK, 77G, or 3OK are already active.

Why the Applications Are Missing

The key lies in understanding the foundational scope item BJ8 (Make-to-Stock Production – Process Manufacturing Based on Process Order).

  • The technical business catalog SAP_SCM_BC_PROC_ORD_MGMT_MC (Process Order Management) is delivered through BJ8.

  • Specialized scope items such as 42N, 3UK, 77G, or 3OK depend on BJ8 to establish the underlying process manufacturing framework.

  • If BJ8 is not activated in your Starter environment, the catalog cannot be pulled into your assigned business roles, which explains why the apps are missing.

How to Enable the Applications

To make these applications available for your workshops or productive use, follow these steps:

  • Activate Scope Item BJ8 Once BJ8 is active, the catalog SAP_SCM_BC_PROC_ORD_MGMT_MC will be included in the standard business roles.

  • Assign Business Catalogs If you are using a customized business role, ensure that the catalog is manually assigned to the role.

  • Verify Applications After completing the above steps, you will be able to search and access the relevant process order applications in your system.

Reference

For more details, consult the Process Navigator: BJ8, which provides comprehensive guidance on scope item activation and dependencies.

entry.title: SAP Business AI | What | How | Insights
entry.content:
SAP Business AI

SAP Business AI is a comprehensive suite of artificial intelligence technologies and capabilities embedded directly into SAP's enterprise applications and business processes. It represents SAP's strategic approach to delivering relevant, reliable, and responsible AI that transforms how businesses operate by providing intelligent automation, predictive insights, and enhanced decision-making capabilities across the entire enterprise.

Key Components

  • Joule: SAP's AI-powered copilot that provides a unified, conversational interface for interacting with SAP applications using natural language.
  • Embedded AI: AI capabilities natively integrated into core SAP solutions like S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba, providing out-of-the-box functionality.
  • AI Foundation: Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), providing the underlying infrastructure for AI development, deployment, and governance.
  • Joule Agents: Specialized AI agents that handle complex, cross-functional business tasks autonomously or collaboratively.

Primary Capabilities

  • Intelligent Automation: Automates repetitive tasks like invoice processing, document analysis, and workflow management using machine learning and robotic process automation.
  • Predictive Analytics: Leverages machine learning algorithms to forecast trends, predict demand, and enable proactive decision-making across business functions.
  • Natural Language Processing: Enables conversational interactions, document processing, and intelligent content generation through advanced NLP capabilities.
  • Generative AI: Creates new content, summarizes complex information, generates code, and provides creative solutions for business challenges.

Integration with SAP Ecosystem

  • Deep Application Integration: AI capabilities are embedded across SAP's entire product portfolio including S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba, and SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Unified Data Foundation: Built on SAP BTP with services like SAP Datasphere and Integration Suite to ensure high-quality data access for AI algorithms.
  • Cross-Application Workflows: Joule provides seamless navigation and task execution across multiple SAP applications through a single interface.
  • Strategic Partnerships: Collaborates with leading AI vendors like Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM to leverage cutting-edge technologies and large language models.

Use Cases

  • Finance: Automated invoice processing, cash forecasting, financial reconciliation, and predictive analytics for better financial planning .
  • Supply Chain: Demand forecasting, predictive material requirements planning, and intelligent supply chain optimization.
  • Human Resources: Resume screening, job description generation, personalized learning recommendations, and workforce planning.
  • Procurement: Intelligent spend analysis, contract intelligence, supplier risk detection, and automated sourcing processes .
  • Customer Experience: Personalized recommendations, intelligent customer service, and enhanced user experiences across touchpoints .

Some useful resources

discovering-sap-business-ai

Business AI & Joule

ai-catalog

entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.4.

You can find this preview on the current SAP Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.4”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Re: Clarifications on HANA DB access in SAP Private Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi @Nishatharu_Weerakkodi ,

Thank you for the detailed description of your scenario. Let me address each of your points regarding HANA DB access post RISE migration.

1. Direct HANA DB Access in RISE (Private Cloud)
In SAP RISE Private Cloud, direct HANA DB access is significantly restricted compared to On-Premises. SAP manages the underlying infrastructure, and by default, customer created HANA DB users with direct DB access are not carried over or supported in the same way post-migration. SAP's standard position is that direct HANA DB access is limited to specific technical/schema users managed by SAP Basis.

2. Calculation Views & Analysis for Office (AFO)
The good news is that Calculation Views can still be used in S/4HANA 2025. However, the access method changes:
- Direct HANA user login to AFO will likely not be supported in the same way
- SAP recommends using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) or BW/4HANA as the analytical layer instead
- If direct HANA access is still required, you need to raise a request with SAP to provision specific DB users — this is handled by SAP Basis in RISE and is subject to SAP's approval and security policies
- The 10 custom HANA user IDs and their Calculation View authorizations will not automatically migrate — these need to be re-evaluated and re-provisioned

3. MS Fabric Integration via HANA ODBC
This is the most impacted area. In RISE Private Cloud:
- Direct HANA ODBC connections from external tools like MS Fabric are generally not supported out of the box
- The On-Premises Data Gateway approach will need to be re-architected
- SAP recommends using SAP Datasphere (formerly Data Warehouse Cloud) or OData APIs as the integration layer for external tools
- You will need to work with SAP to evaluate if a read-only technical DB user can be provisioned for this use case, but this is not guaranteed

4. Authorizations Post Migration
Even if user IDs are retained or re-created, authorizations do not automatically carry over. HANA roles and privileges need to be re-assigned in the new system, especially since the schema structure may change between Suite on HANA and S/4HANA 2025.

Recommended Next Steps:
- Raise a formal request with your SAP RISE contract team / Cloud Delivery team to clarify what level of HANA DB access is permitted under your contract
- Engage SAP Early Adopter Care or your SAP Account Executive to discuss the analytical access requirements before migration
- Evaluate SAP Datasphere as a replacement for MS Fabric's direct HANA connection
- Plan for AFO to connect via SAC Live Connection or BW queries rather than direct HANA user login

Hope this helps clarify the impact. I would strongly recommend aligning with SAP on these points early in your migration project to avoid surprises post go-live.

Regards,

Naveen

entry.title: Re: 销售高级首付款申请
entry.content:
Currently there is product limit for region TW adoption the ADP process:

ADP is a new prepayment processing approach introduced by SAP (scope item 7S7, billing type DPR1) that stores prepayment data in a new internal data structure. At this time, no Taiwan enhancements exist for this new flow, and the Taiwan e-invoice XML mapping logic has not yet been adapted to the new structure.

Following KBA: 3754961 - eDocument Taiwan: XML Missing Down Payment Information in Advanced Down Payment Process. You can access it here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3754961.

entry.title: Receive customer payment on behalf of using Advanced Payment Management and In House Banking
entry.content:
Hi All,

My client has a requirement for Receipt on behalf of (ROBO) as below

  1. Company code in UK (Subsidiary 1) invoices a customer in USA.
  2. US Customer makes payment to the bank account of Company code in USA (Susidiary 2).
  3. USA company code posts the bank statement and manually reconcile the customer open item in NL company code via journal.  

We are trying to evaluate if APM with IHB can handle this requirement.

In IHB we have payment scenarios as below which can handle payment via Inhouse bank owner (i.e. corporate group), but our requirement is to receive the payment in US Subsidiary on behalf of NL subsidiary without the involvement of the corporate group.

  1. Central Incoming Payments - Central incoming payments refer to the scenario in which payments for subsidiaries are received via a bank statement of an external bank account belonging to the corporate group that is the owner of the in-house bank
  2. Intercompany Payments - Intercompany payments refer to the scenario in which subsidiary 1 wants to pay an invoice via its in-house bank account to another in-house bank account owned by subsidiary 2

Can we handle this requirement in IHB & APM?

entry.title: Re: FULL list of Payment terms for Customer
entry.content:
I think you can use "Custom CDS View" app to list all the payment terms registered in your environment. 
You may use data sources like I_PaymentTerms (primary data source) and I_PaymentTermsText (secondary data source, cardinality is Zero or One [0..1]). 
In the "elements" section, you can choose what value to display in the CDS view. 

Once you publish the CDS, you are able to export into excel. 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: MDK iOS for rookie
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am a complete beginner when it comes to iOS. I’ve never owned an iPhone and have never developed in Xcode. All my previous projects have been in the web and Android domains.

After developing an SAP MDK app and testing it on Android, I’ve been asked to distribute it to iPhone users.

As I understand, the first step is to create a Signing Profile for Cloud Build. This is straightforward for Android, but for iOS it requires uploading a Signing Certificate and a Provisioning Profile from Apple.

This is where the nightmare begins. Is it possible to proceed without having an iOS device at all? I do have users with iPhones who can help with testing, but I’m not sure how to actually distribute the app.

Any help, guidance, or references would be highly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Microsoft GRAPH API - Read and extract attachments of Outlook email from Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Santosh,

You can connect SAP CPI to cloud Microsoft Outlook / Microsoft 365 using the Mail adapter via IMAP.

Since you are migrating to the cloud, you can replace your inbound Mail/IMAP sender adapter with an integration flow (iFlow) that connects directly to the Microsoft Graph API. If still you wanted to use Microsoft Graph API, even that is also possible.

entry.title: SAP WM Public Cloud - Picking Outbound WT split at Bulk Storage Type
entry.content:
Hello community,
In SAP WM Public Cloud, is it not possible to automatically split tasks between Bulk and Picking areas?
I have configured palletization in the material, unit classification, and quantity classifier in the Storage Types, but the behavior is not as expected.
Scenarios with 100 kg palletization.
1) Outbound with 10 kg -> Creates a 10 kg WT from the Picking Storage Type [OK]
2) Outbound with 100 kg -> Creates a 100 kg WT from the Bulk Storage Type [OK]
3) Outbound with 110kg -> Creates a 110kg WT from the Bulk Storage Type [NOK] 

The expectation is to create a WT of 100 kg from Bulk Storage and 10 kg from picking.

In the Bulk Storage Type, I have the setting: “Round down to a multiple of a unit”.
If I check the WT creation log, I see the message: “Requested quantity cannot be rounded”.

Is it not possible to perform the automatic split between Bulk and Picking?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP CPQ技術ブログ①: Involved Parties の Sold-To Party 名を Quote List に表示する方法
entry.content:
概要

SAP CPQ のデフォルトの Quote List には、Involved Parties で設定した Sold-To Party (受注先) の Business Partner 名が列として表示されません。 本記事では、Quote カスタムフィールドと Global Script を組み合わせることで、この情報を Quote List 上に表示する手順を解説します。

対象バージョン: SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 エンジン (CPQ 2605 以降で動作確認)

アーキテクチャ概要

実現方法の全体像は以下の通りです。

コンポーネント 役割

Quote カスタムフィールド SoldToPartyNameSold-To Party 名を格納する FreeForm フィールド
Quote List Settings上記フィールドを列として Quote List に表示する設定
Global Script SoldToPartyNameInvolved Parties 変更時に Sold-To Party 名を読み取りカスタムフィールドへ書き込む IronPython スクリプト

図 1: 完成形 — Quote List の "Sold-To Party" 列に Business Partner 名 "Alpha Center" が表示されている

 

 

手順

Step 1: Quote カスタムフィールドを作成する

Setup > Quotes > Quote Fields を開き、「Custom Fields」タブで新規フィールドを作成します。

設定項目 値

NameSoldToPartyName
TypeFreeForm
Default LabelSold-To Party
Calculations are run「On Involved Parties Changed」にチェックを入れる
Formula (Implementation)空欄のまま ※後述の Global Script で値を設定するため

 

図 2: カスタムフィールド設定画面 — Type: FreeForm、"On Involved Parties Changed" にチェック、Formula は空欄

 

Step 2: Quote List Settings に列を追加する

Setup > Quotes > Quote List Settings を開き、「Columns Layout」セクションで手順 1 で作成した SoldToPartyName フィールドを選択して追加します。

図 3: Quote List Settings の Columns Layout に "Sold-To Party" を追加した状態

 

ポイント: 列ラベルはフィールドの「Default Label」で設定した文字列 (Sold-To Party) が使われます。ユーザータイプ別にラベルを変えたい場合は「User Types By Labels」で設定してください。

Step 3: Global Script を作成する

Setup > Develop > Global Scripts を開き、「Add Script」で新規スクリプトを作成します。

設定項目 値

NameSoldToPartyName（任意）
System ID一意な ID（例: SoldToPartyName_cpq）※自動設定
Activeチェックを入れる

「Script Code」欄に以下のコードを入力し、Save してください。

# Sold-To Party の Business Partner 名を取得し、カスタムフィールドに書き込む
soldToName = ""
for party in context.Quote.GetInvolvedParties():
    if party.PartnerFunctionName == "Sold-to party":
        soldToName = party.Name or ""
        break
context.Quote["SoldToPartyName"] = soldToName

図 4: Global Script のコード入力画面 — Quote 2.0 API を使用したスクリプト

 

スクリプトの解説

コード 説明

context.Quote.GetInvolvedParties()Quote 2.0 API。Involved Parties に登録された全パーティを返す
party.PartnerFunctionNameパートナー機能名（例: "Sold-to party"）
party.NameBusiness Partner の名称（例: "Alpha Center"）
context.Quote["SoldToPartyName"]Quote 2.0 でカスタムフィールドに値をセットする記法

 

Save 後、ページをリロードして Events タブを開きます。

図 5: Global Script の Events タブ — "On Involved Parties Changed" イベントにチェックを入れる

 

「On Involved Parties Changed」にチェックを入れ、Save してください。これにより Involved Parties が変更されるたびにこのスクリプトが自動実行されます。

Step 4: 動作確認

  1. 任意の Quote を開き、「Involved parties」タブに遷移します。
  2. Sold-to party として任意の Business Partner を設定し、Save します。
  3. Quote List に戻ると、「Sold-To Party」列に Business Partner 名が表示されます。

図 6: Quote の Involved Parties タブ — ここで Sold-to party を設定することでスクリプトがトリガーされる

 

図 7: Quote List での確認結果 — Quote 02080027 の "Sold-To Party" 列に "Alpha Center" が表示されている

 

まとめ

本記事では、SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 環境において Involved Parties の Sold-To Party 名を Quote List に表示する方法を解説しました。要点は以下の 3 点です。

  1. FreeForm カスタムフィールドを作成し、Quote List の列に追加する
  2. Global Script に Quote 2.0 API (context.Quote.GetInvolvedParties()) を使ったスクリプトを記述する
  3. Global Script の Events タブで「On Involved Parties Changed」を有効化する

ポイント: 同じ手法を応用すれば、Ship-To Party や Bill-To Party など他のパートナー機能名を Quote List に表示することも可能です。party.PartnerFunctionName の比較文字列を変更するだけで対応できます。

参考リンク

  • SAP CPQ Scripting Interfaces ドキュメント (2605)
  • SAP CPQ Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SQLMD: Cross Node Joins Analysis
entry.content: SQLM Cross Node Joins refers to nodes as host. M_NUMA_NODES refers to numa nodes which is the internal architecture of the server. So you must not mix this up. The "node" (aka host) can be queried from M_CS_TABLES.

entry.title: Re: how to get CacheManager from customcommercewebservice...
entry.content:
Hi, I hit the same issue during our JDK21 upgrade. Ehcache 3 dropped the global CacheManager.getCacheManager(name) registry, so name-based lookup is gone for good. You have to retrieve it as a Spring bean instead. Here's the pattern that worked for us:

1. Don't keep the old defaultWSEhcache / defaultWSCacheManager block in the web extension's cache-config-spring.xml.

Those beans rely on org.springframework.cache.ehcache.EhCacheCacheManager and TenantAwareEhCacheManagerFactoryBean, both Ehcache 2 only. We removed (commented out) the whole block in every custom override of the OOTB *webservices cache configs.

2. Define a single cache manager at the custom extension level using Spring, JSR-107 (JCache) and Ehcache 3:

<bean id="myWSCacheManager" class="org.springframework.cache.jcache.JCacheCacheManager">
<property name="cacheManager" ref="myWSEhcache"/>
</bean>

<bean id="myWSEhcache" class="org.springframework.cache.jcache.JCacheManagerFactoryBean">
<property name="cacheManagerUri" value="classpath:ehcache.xml"/>
</bean>

Cache regions and TTLs live in ehcache.xml using the Ehcache 3 schema (<cache alias="..."><expiry><ttl unit="seconds">...</ttl></expiry></cache>). TTL is enforced transparently, no more Element.setTimeToLive(...) in Java.

3. Inject with an explicit @Qualifier in your eviction code.

Plain @Autowired CacheManager will throw NoUniqueBeanDefinitionException because there are usually multiple cache manager beans in the context.

@Resource
@Qualifier("myWSCacheManager")
private org.springframework.cache.CacheManager cacheManager;

public void evict(String cacheName, String key) {
org.springframework.cache.Cache cache = cacheManager.getCache(cacheName);
if (cache != null) cache.evict(key);
}

Stick to Spring's org.springframework.cache.CacheManager interface, don't reach for the raw org.ehcache.CacheManager. Keeps you decoupled from the impl and consistent with what Spring 6 expects.

4. Cross-extension visibility.

Web extensions' WEB-INF Spring context is a child of the platform context, so beans defined in a custom extension are visible from the web layer. Just make sure your web extension declares <requires-extension name="..."/> to whichever custom extension owns the cache manager.

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: Compare roles across system
entry.content:
Error between ABAP 7.51 and ABAP 7.58, fixed by applying this note:

3443222 - RSUSR050 | Error during remote comparison with "Fast Serialization" option - SAP for Me

You use the report RSUSR050 to remotely compare users, roles, or profiles.

If an RFC-specific error occurs during the comparison, the user receives only the cryptic error message I::000.
This error occurs as soon as an RFC destination is used when selecting the transfer protocol with the special option "Fast Serialization".
This is newer RFC connection technology.

Solution

Implement the attached correction using SNOTE or by importing the referenced support package.

entry.title: Re: Custom Section with Rating Scale in SAP SuccessFactors Performance Form
entry.content: Hello, If you are using a custom section, you can create a custom element of type "list" and add each rating value as picklist option. This approach is suitable when the rating is used only for informational purposes and is not involved in any calculations. However if the rating needs to be used in summary calculations, you should use a standard section and map it to the appropriate rating scale.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce JDK & Spring Framework Upgrade
entry.content:
Hi Jagan, the honest answer is: it depends on the scope and customization level of your project. For a near-vanilla SAP Commerce project with very few custom extensions and limited third-party integrations, 2 to 3 weeks with two developers is achievable, especially if you lean on automated refactoring tools. For a moderately or heavily customized project, it's not realistic. Here's the scope you should map against your codebase before committing to a timeline:

  1. Jakarta namespace migration. Every javax.* import has to move to jakarta.*, which cascades through controllers, filters, interceptors, web service clients, and any third-party library that hasn't moved yet.
  2. Spring 6 / Spring Security 6 API removals. WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter is gone, path matching defaults changed, several security and cache APIs were tightened.
  3. Ehcache 2 to Ehcache 3 / JCache. Different XML schema, different programmatic API, no more global CacheManager.getCacheManager(name) lookup. Any custom caching or eviction code needs a rewrite.
  4. Third-party library upgrades. Many common libraries need version bumps for JDK 21 compatibility, several with breaking APIs. JDK 21 also breaks reflection-heavy libs that need either an upgrade or --add-opens flags.
  5. SAP Commerce OOTB re-merge. Any OOTB extension you've customized needs to be re-diffed against the new baseline. SAP shipped non-trivial changes across many platform extensions for the JDK21-compatible release.
  6. Build/runtime config and full regression test surface. Tomcat 10, build script changes, CCv2 aspect tuning, plus retesting storefronts, REST APIs, cockpits, cronjobs, and integrations.

One tip if your project is lightly customized: tools like OpenRewrite (https://docs.openrewrite.org/) have ready-made recipes for javax-to-jakarta, Spring Boot/Spring 6 upgrades, JUnit 4 to 5, and several deprecated-API replacements. For a near-vanilla codebase, OpenRewrite can knock out a large chunk of the mechanical refactoring in a day or two, which makes a short timeline realistic. For heavily customized projects it still helps, but you'll spend most of your time on the parts the recipes can't touch (Ehcache, OOTB extension re-merges, third-party libs with no recipe, integration code).

One important warning: SAP is still actively patching the JDK21-compatible 2211 releases. Between the first JDK21-capable patch and the latest (now is jdk21.11), there have been many fixes and small behavior changes. Don't pick a target version and freeze. Read the release notes for every patch between your starting point and your target, and re-test the affected areas after each bump. Some issues are only documented in a single patch's release note.

Rough realistic estimates by scope:
- Near-vanilla project, few custom extensions, no third-party integrations: 2 to 3 weeks with OpenRewrite + 1 week of QA.
- Moderately customized project: 2 to 3 months of focused work for two developers, plus a full QA cycle.
- Heavily customized project (custom auth, custom caching, custom integrations, many OOTB overrides): 3 to 4 months.

The best way to lock down a real number is to do a 2 to 3 day spike: run OpenRewrite, count compile errors, list third-party libs that fail to resolve, and audit which OOTB extensions you've overridden. That gives you a defensible estimate instead of a guess.

Hope that helps with your planning. 

Best regards

Astro

entry.title: Re: Download TAN Exemption Details
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

Please note that in the Mass Upload Functionality of TAN Exemption Details window, you must select the Download radio button and maintain all the required fields, including File Name, Company Code, and Supplier. Once executed, the suppliers’ certificate details maintained in the Business Partner master data can be downloaded through the 'TAN Exemption Mass Upload' application in XLS format. 

Kindly refer the below help document for more information,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/ab301e11846d4175aa1704c07a5238c7.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: PO Line Item Discount Separate G/L Posting During Supplier Invoice
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

I would like to seek your guidance regarding supplier invoice posting behavior in SAP. Currently, when I apply a PO line item discount in a purchase order, the system only reduces the net purchase/invoice value, and no separate G/L posting is generated for the discount amount during supplier invoice posting.

My requirement is to post the discount amount to a separate G/L account (e.g., a purchase discount account). Example of accounting entry expected:

  • Dr. Expense
  •     Cr. AP
  •     Cr. Purchase Discount

I have tried creating a custom condition type and maintaining the account mapping in SSCUI “Set Calculation Schema - Purchasing”, but the system still does not trigger any separate posting for the discount amount during Supplier Invoice posting.

May I know whether there is any standard SAP configuration or recommended best practice to achieve this requirement? If separate posting for PO discounts is not standard SAP behavior, what would be the recommended alternative approach?

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Best regards,

HY

entry.title: cross model copy Copy rules-issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While copying the data from one model to another model using data action cross model copy step, I am facing an issue. every day new master data (Deals) and its transaction data is created in the source model. Target model doesn't have this new master data which is causing the data action to throw errors (Check the attachment). Everyday I need to maintain the copy rules in the data action after copying the newly created master data in the target model. These dimensions are not public dimensions. Is there any better way to handle this? please suggest.

Thanks

Sridhar

entry.title: Why Businesses Prefer Appinventiv as Their Product Engineering Company
entry.content:
In today’s fast-moving digital market, businesses need scalable and innovative digital solutions to stay competitive. That’s why choosing the right product engineering company has become a major business decision for startups and enterprises alike.

Appinventiv  is widely recognized for helping businesses build high-performing digital products across industries like healthcare, fintech, retail, education, and logistics. As a leading product engineering company, Appinventiv focuses on end-to-end product development — from idea validation and UI/UX design to development, testing, deployment, and post-launch support.

One of the biggest advantages of working with Appinventiv is its expertise in emerging technologies such as AI, cloud computing, blockchain, IoT, and data analytics. Their engineering teams help businesses accelerate digital transformation while reducing time-to-market and development risks.

A reliable product engineering company not only develops applications but also ensures scalability, security, and long-term product success. Appinventiv has successfully delivered solutions for startups, enterprises, and Fortune 500 brands by combining innovation with business-focused strategies.

For businesses planning to build future-ready digital products, partnering with an experienced product engineering company like Appinventiv can provide a strong competitive advantage.

entry.title: Data transfer between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Databricks – guidance and issue clarification
entry.content:
Data transfer between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Databricks – guidance and issue clarification. We require guidance on enabling data exchange between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and the Databricks platform.

We specifically need step-by-step guidance and supported approaches for the following:

How to send data from SAC to Databricks and How to send data from Databricks into SAC 

Standard and supported methods for both directions (SAC → Databricks and Databricks → SAC).

entry.title: Object Oriented ABAP in SAP!!
entry.content:
The below Concepts have been covered in this blog:

  • Types of Classes
  • Difference between Procedural ABAP and OO ABAP
  • Types of Methods
  • Visibility
  • Instance Object and Method
  • Static Method
  • Constructor
  • Events
  • Interfaces
  • Polymorphism
  • Inheritance
  • ABSTRACT Class
  • SINGLETON Class
  • Persistent Class
  • Exception Class
  • Unit Test Class

What is OOPS ?

OOPS Based on Class and Objects.

Class is a Collection of various Objects like Methods, Attributes, Events, Interfaces.

Object is an Instance of a Class.

Types of Classes:

Global Class: We can reuse the class. Created in TCode se24.

Types of Global classes are

  • Normal Class: Purpose is to write the logic
  • Exception Class: Purpose is to Raise and handle the Exceptions
  • Persistence Class: Purpose is for Database Operations
  • Unit Test Class: Purpose is to write Unit Test cases

Local Class:  Created in TCode se38. Dedicated to the Program only.

Difference between Procedural ABAP and OO ABAP:

Procedural ABAP:

  • Use Subroutines, Function Modules.
  • Code reusability is limited compared to OO ABAP.

OO ABAP:

  • Use Methods, Classes.
  • Code reusability is high through Inheritance.
  • Easier to maintain and modify due to Modularity and Encapsulation.

Types of Methods:

  • Instance Method: Need Object to call the Method.

                                 DATA: lo_object TYPE REF TO CLASSNAME.

                                 CREATE OBJECT lo_object.

                                 CALL METHOD lo_object->Method_name.

Practical Example in Web UI: I have published a blog on creating Tree View in SAP CRM Web UI. Here I have used Instance Method and called it using an Object. Link is given below for ready reference.

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-members/detailed-steps-to-create-tree-view-in-sap-crm-web-ui/ba-p/13439626 

  • Static Method: No need of Object to call the Method. Method can be called directly with Class Name itself. CALL METHOD ClassName=>Method_name.

Practical Example in Web UI: I have published blog on Service Due List Indicator. Here I have called the Static method SAP_ICON of Class CL_BSP_MIMES.

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-members/service-due-indicator-for-managers/ba-p/13572219 

Visibility:

  • Public: Can be accessed within the Class, Outside the Class and Subclasses.
  • Private:  Can be accessed only within the Class.
  • Protected:  Can be accessed within the Class and the Subclass.

Create a Local Class in a Program:

  • We give the CLASS-DEFINITION and CLASS-IMPLEMENTATION in the Program.

Comparison between class created in se24 and Local Class:

INSTANCE Object & METHOD:

  • METHOD Defined Using METHODS in Program.
  • DATA Defined Using DATA in Program.
  • Can access Instance Attributes, Instance Methods, Static Attributes and Static Methods of the Class.

Static Method:

  • METHOD Defined Using CLASS-METHODS in Program.
  • DATA Defined Using CLASS-DATA in Program.
  • Can access Static Attributes and Static Methods of the Class.
  • Cannot access the Instance Attributes, Instance Methods of the Class

INSTANCE Object & METHOD:

Practical Example of Instance Attribute in Web UI:

Blog: I have published a blog on Segmentation using Attributes in SAP CRM.

In this blog, the Communication Channel was passed as ‘08’ in the Event Handler EH_ONEXPORT of the Component/View SEGFPR_STC/VSMain. The Event Handler has been called from DO_HANDLE_EVENT method of the Controller using the Instance attribute of the Interface.

The blog has been given below for your ready reference:

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-members/segmentation-using-attributes-in-sap-crm/ba-p/13484068 

Method and Data Declarations:

Below screenshot has declaration of both Instance Attribute and Static Attribute.

Equivalent of Method and Data Declarations in a Local Program is given below.

Method Implementation in se24:

Equivalent of Method implementation (in se24) in a Local Program is given below.

Calling the method of class created in se24 from program is given below:

Equivalent of above screenshot for Local class in a Local Program is given below in Highlighted section.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZTEST_LOCALCLASS
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZTEST_LOCALCLASS.
PARAMETERS : p_inv_no       TYPE NUMC5.
DATA       : LV_INV_NAME    TYPE CHAR30,
             LV_DISP_NAME   TYPE CHAR100.
* Class Definition
  CLASS class_prog DEFINITION.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
      DATA :       INS_ATTR_NAME TYPE CHAR50. " Instance Attribute
      CLASS-DATA : STA_ATTR_NAME TYPE CHAR50. " Static Attribute
*     Method Definition
      METHODS INV_DISPLAY IMPORTING IMP_INV_NO   TYPE NUMC5
                          EXPORTING EXP_INV_NAME TYPE CHAR30.
  ENDCLASS.
*Class Implementation
 CLASS class_prog IMPLEMENTATION.
*   Method Implementation
    METHOD INV_DISPLAY.
         SELECT SINGLE INV_NAME INTO LV_INV_NAME FROM ZINV_DET
                 WHERE INV_NO = IMP_INV_NO.
    ENDMETHOD.                        "End of METHOD INV_DISPLAY.
 ENDCLASS.
 START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA : LO_class_prog TYPE REF TO class_prog.
    CREATE OBJECT lo_class_prog.
*   Calling the Method of the Class
    CALL METHOD lo_class_prog->INV_DISPLAY
     EXPORTING
       imp_inv_no   = P_INV_NO
     IMPORTING
       exp_inv_name = LV_INV_NAME.
*  Assignin the values to the Attributes.
*  The Instance Object can acess both the Static Attribute and Instance 
*  Attribute.
   LO_CLASS_PROG->INS_ATTR_NAME = 'Instance Attribute Value'. 
"  Assigning value to Instance Attribute
   LO_CLASS_PROG->STA_ATTR_NAME = 'Static Attribute Value'.   
"  Assigning value to Static Attribute
   CONCATENATE LO_CLASS_PROG->INS_ATTR_NAME SPACE LO_CLASS_PROG->STA_ATTR_NAME SPACE LV_INV_NAME INTO LV_DISP_NAME.
   WRITE : / 'The Name is ', LV_DISP_NAME.

Static Method:

  • METHOD Defined Using CLASS-METHODS in Program.
  • DATA Defined Using CLASS-DATA in Program.
  • Can access Static Attributes and Static Methods of the Class.
  • Cannot access the Instance Attributes, Instance Methods of the Class

Method and Data Declarations:

Equivalent of the above declarations is given below:

Method Definition:

Equivalent of the above Method Definition is given below:

Calling the method of class created in se24 from program is given below:

Equivalent of the above in a Local Program is given below:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZTEST_LOCALCLASS_STATIC
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZTEST_LOCALCLASS_STATIC.
PARAMETERS : p_no       TYPE NUMC5.
* Data Declaration
  DATA       : LV_NAME    TYPE CHAR100,
               LV_DISP_NAME   TYPE CHAR100.
* Class Definition
  CLASS class_Sta_prog DEFINITION.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
      CLASS-DATA : STA_ATTR_NAME TYPE CHAR50. " Static Attribute
*     Static Method Definition
      CLASS-METHODS   STA_DISPLAY IMPORTING IMP_INV_NO   TYPE NUMC5
                                  EXPORTING EXP_INV_NAME TYPE CHAR30.
  ENDCLASS.
  CLASS class_Sta_prog IMPLEMENTATION.
      METHOD STA_DISPLAY.
         SELECT SINGLE INV_NAME INTO LV_NAME FROM ZINV_DET
                 WHERE INV_NO = p_no.
      ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.
  START-OF-SELECTION.
      zcl_se24class=>STA_ATTR_NAME = 'Static Attribute Name'.
      CALL METHOD zcl_se24class=>sta_display
        EXPORTING
          imp_sta_no   = p_no
        IMPORTING
          exp_sta_name = lv_name.
"      Static Class cannot access Instance Attributes
*      zcl_se24class=>INS_ATTR_NAME = 'Instance Attribute Name'. 
"      Static Class cannot access Instance Method
*      CALL METHOD zcl_se24class=>ins_display                    
*        EXPORTING
*          imp_inv_no   = p_no
*        IMPORTING
*          exp_inv_name = lv_disp_name.
CONCATENATE zcl_se24class=>STA_ATTR_NAME SPACE LV_NAME INTO               LV_DISP_NAME.
       WRITE : / 'The Name is ', LV_DISP_NAME.

CONSTRUCTOR:

Constructor is a Special Type of Method that is executed Automatically. We will not be explicitly calling the Constructor.

They are method with a predefined name and cannot be called using Call Method Statement.

Two Types of Constructors are available in sap.

They are

  • Constructor / Instance Constructor: Called Everytime when the Class Object is Created.
  • CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR / Static Constructor: Called First time the Class is accessed.

Multiple CONSTRUCTOR are not allowed in a class.

Multiple CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR are not allowed in a class.

INSTANCE CONSTRUCTOR:

  • Called Everytime an Object is Created.
  • Can access both Static and Instance Attributes
  • Can have Importing Parameters.
  • Name of the Method: CONSTRUCTOR

STATIC CONSTRUCTOR:

  • Called Only Once the first time the class is accessed.
  • Cannot have Importing Parameters.

TCode SE24:

Program in se38:

Output:

Analysis:

Sequence of Execution:

When the Code to create the Object is called,

  • Static Constructor is called first and then
  • Instance Constructor is called and then
  • Remaining part of the Program is executed.

Output:

Analysis

When the Code to create the Object is called,

  • Static Constructor is called first and then
  • Instance Constructor is called and then
  • Again, the Instance Constructor is called and then
  • Remaining part of the Program is executed.

Attribute Screenshot:

Instance Constructor can access both Instance attribute and Static Attribute:

Static Constructor can access only Static Attribute and cannot access Instance Attribute:

Code is activated after commenting the access to Instance Attribute.

Passing Parameters in a Constructor:

Static Attribute CANNOT Have any Parameters:

Only Instance Constructor can have IMPORT Parameters. (ONLY IMPORT Parameters)

The Instance Constructor can access both Static Attribute and Instance Attribute.

Passing values to Instance Constructor:

Analysis:

  1. Once the Create Object code in the Program is executed the Static Constructor Class CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR is executed. The WRITE Statement in the CONSTRUCTOR method is executed.
  2. Then the Instance Constructor Class CONSTRUCTOR is called and the values from the Parameter are passed to the Importing Parameters of the Instance Constructor.
  3. In the Instance Constructor Class CONSTRUCTOR the Import Parameters values are assigned to the Static and Instance Attributes of the Class
  4. The WRITE statement in the Instance Constructor is Executed.
  5. When the Create Object code in the Program is executed once again, then the above steps (b) , (c) and (d) are executed again. (Please Note:  The Static Constructor Class CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR is not called again as it is called only the first time the class is accessed).
  6. The WRITE statement in the report program is Executed.

Creating Constructor in Local Class of a Program:

Output:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZTEST_CONSTRUCTOR_LOCAL
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZTEST_CONSTRUCTOR_LOCAL.
PARAMETERS : p_name1 TYPE CHAR6,
             p_name2 TYPE CHAR6.
* Local Class Definition
  CLASS class_cons_LCL DEFINITION.
      PUBLIC SECTION.
           DATA :       INS_ATTR_NAME TYPE CHAR6.    "Instance Attribute
           CLASS-DATA : STA_ATTR_NAME TYPE CHAR6.    "Static Attribute
           METHODS CONSTRUCTOR IMPORTING IMP_NAME1 TYPE CHAR6        "Instance Constructor
                                         IMP_NAME2 TYPE CHAR6.
           CLASS-METHODS CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR.          "Static Constructor
  ENDCLASS.
* Local Class Implementation
  CLASS class_cons_lcl IMPLEMENTATION.
*          Instance Constructor
           METHOD CONSTRUCTOR.
                 INS_ATTR_NAME = IMP_NAME1.
                 STA_ATTR_NAME = IMP_NAME2.
                 WRITE : / 'This is Instance Constructor'.
                 WRITE : / 'The values entered in screen are',INS_ATTR_NAME , 'and' ,STA_ATTR_NAME.
           ENDMETHOD.
*          Static Constructor
           METHOD CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR.
                 STA_ATTR_NAME = p_name1.
*                 INS_ATTR_NAME = p_name2.
                 WRITE : / 'This is Static Constructor'.
                 WRITE : / 'The values entered in screen are', STA_ATTR_NAME.
           ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.
  START-OF-SELECTION.
      DATA : lo_object TYPE REF to class_cons_lcl.
*     Create the Object ( Here both Static Constructor and Instance 
*     Constructor are called ).
      CREATE OBJECT lo_object EXPORTING IMP_NAME1 = p_name1
                                        IMP_NAME2 = p_name2 .
      WRITE : / 'This is a Test message'.

When the Code to create the Object is called,

  • Static Constructor is called first and then
  • Instance Constructor is called and then
  • Remaining part of the Program is executed.

Passing values to CONSTRUCTOR in a Local class of a Program:

The Class Definition made in se24 is made in the Program as per code below:

Instance Constructor Implementation in se24.

Equivalent of above screenshot is given below:

Static Constructor in TCode se24:

Equivalent of above screenshot is given below:

Analysis:

When the Code to create the Object is called,

  • Static Constructor is called first and then
  • Instance Constructor is called and then
  • Remaining part of the Program is executed.

Entire Code is Given Below:

EVENTS:

  • With help of Event a Method of One Class can call the Method of Another Class.
  • Triggering Method is the method that will raise the Event.
  • Event Handler Method is the method that will handle the Event.
  • Using the Method SET HANDLER we register to the Event.

Events can be of Two Types:

  • Instance Event
  • Static Event

Practical Example of Instance Event: I have published a blog to give a Pop Up once the user enters a value in a field in Web UI. The link for this is given below for your ready reference.

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-members/pop-up-to-get-triggerred-once-user-enters-a-value-in-a-field-in-web-ui/ba-p/13601926 

  • In Local class of Program, Instance Event, is declared using EVENT.
  • In Local class of Program, Static Event is declared using CLASS-EVENT.
  • Instance event can access Instance Event, Instance Methods, Static Events , Static Methods.
  • Static Event can access only Static Events and Static Methods.

Analysis:

  • The method ev_meth of Class CLASS_EVENT_RAISE gets called and the Number given in the selection screen is passed to it.
  • If the Input is initial, then the Event NO_INPUT is raised
  • This triggers the method ev_meth_handle of Class CLASS_EVENT_HANDLE
  • In this method ev_meth_handle of Class CLASS_EVENT_HANDLE the Message is displayed
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZTEST_EVENT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZTEST_EVENT.
PARAMETERS : p_no TYPE NUMC5.
* Class Definition of the Class Raising the Event
  CLASS CLASS_EVENT_RAISE DEFINITION.
       PUBLIC SECTION.
       METHODS ev_meth IMPORTING imp_no    TYPE NUMC5
                      EXPORTING  exp_name  TYPE CHAR30.
       EVENTS : NO_INPUT.
  ENDCLASS.
* Class Definition of the Class handling the Event
  CLASS CLASS_EVENT_HANDLE DEFINITION.
       PUBLIC SECTION.
         METHODS ev_meth_handle for EVENT NO_INPUT of CLASS_EVENT_RAISE.
  ENDCLASS.
* Implmentation of the Class raising the Event
  CLASS CLASS_EVENT_RAISE IMPLEMENTATION.
       METHOD ev_meth.
          IF p_no IS INITIAL.
             RAISE EVENT NO_INPUT.
          ELSE.
              SELECT SINGLE INV_NAME INTO exp_name FROM ZINV_DET
                                     WHERE INV_NO = imp_no.
          ENDIF.
       ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.
* Implmentation of the Class handling the Event
  CLASS CLASS_EVENT_HANDLE IMPLEMENTATION.
       METHOD ev_meth_handle.
            MESSAGE 'Please provide Input' TYPE 'E'.
       ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
   DATA : lo_event_raise   TYPE REF TO CLASS_EVENT_RAISE,
          lo_event_handle  TYPE REF TO CLASS_EVENT_HANDLE.
   DATA : lv_name TYPE CHAR30.
   CREATE OBJECT lo_event_raise.
   CREATE OBJECT lo_event_handle.
*  Registering to the Event
   SET HANDLER lo_EVENT_HANDLE->ev_meth_handle for LO_EVENT_RAISE.
*  Calling the method that would raise event incase the Input is not there
   CALL METHOD lo_event_raise->ev_meth EXPORTING imp_no   = p_no
                                       IMPORTING exp_name = lv_name.
   WRITE : / 'The Name is ' , lv_name.

Accessing Events from se38:

Program in se38:

Output:

Analysis:

  • The Public event are registered in SET HANDLER statement in Program
  • The Private and Protected events are registered in SET HANDLER statement in the class
  • When the RAISE EVENT method is triggered from the program , the Protected and Private events are registered in the method.
  • When the Events are raised the respective method of that event are executed and the Output is displayed.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZPROG_EVENTS
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZPROG_EVENTS.
DATA : lo_object TYPE REF TO ZCL_EVENTS.
* Create the Object
  CREATE OBJECT lo_object.
* Register to the Public Event
  SET HANDLER lo_object->MET_PUB FOR lo_object.
  SET HANDLER lo_object->MET_PUB_ONE.
* SET HANDLER lo_object->MET_PRO FOR ALL INSTANCES.   "Registering of Protected Event outside the Class is not allowed
* SET HANDLER lo_object->MET_PRI FOR ALL INSTANCES.   "Registering of Private Event outside the Class is not allowed
  lo_object->RAISE_EVENT( ).

INTERFACES:

  • In Interfaces all methods are PUBLIC by DEFAULT.
  • Interface methods are all ABSTRACT methods ( There is only Definition and there is no Implementation ).
  • Interface can be used in class and code can be written in the Interface.
  • If the same Interface is used in difference class then the Logic of the code can be different in each of the class. So Interface Exhibits Polymorphism in this case.
  • We can create Interface in se24 / in the program via se38.

Polymorphism:

  • Polymorphism is using same method but different functionality. It allows you to call the same method name on different objects and have each object respond with its own specific behavior.
  • It is a characteristic of being able to assign a different behaviour in a subclass to something that was declared in parent class.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZPROG_INTERFACE
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZPROG_INTERFACE_POLY.

DATA :       lv_output  TYPE CHAR30,
             lv_output1 TYPE CHAR30.
PARAMETERS : p_inv_no TYPE NUMC5.
* Interfae Definition
  INTERFACE INTERFACE1.
    METHODS DISPLAY IMPORTING im_invno  TYPE numc5
                    EXPORTING ex_output TYPE CHAR30.
  ENDINTERFACE.
* Class1 Definition
  CLASS CLASS1 DEFINITION.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
       INTERFACES INTERFACE1.
    ENDCLASS.

* Class2 Definition
  CLASS CLASS2 DEFINITION.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
       INTERFACES INTERFACE1.
    ENDCLASS.

* Class1 Implementation
  CLASS CLASS1 IMPLEMENTATION.
    METHOD INTERFACE1~DISPLAY.
          SELECT SINGLE INV_NAME FROM ZINV_DET INTO ex_output
                                        WHERE inv_no = im_invno.
    ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.

* Class2 Implementation
  CLASS CLASS2 IMPLEMENTATION.
    METHOD INTERFACE1~DISPLAY.
          SELECT SINGLE INV_INDUSTRY FROM ZINV_DET INTO ex_output
                                        WHERE inv_no = im_invno.
    ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
DATA : lo_class1  TYPE REF TO CLASS1,
       lo_class2  TYPE REF TO CLASS2.
   CREATE OBJECT lo_class1.
   CREATE OBJECT lo_class2.
*  Call the method by passing the values to the first class
   CALL METHOD lo_class1->INTERFACE1~DISPLAY
               EXPORTING im_invno   = p_inv_no
               IMPORTING ex_output = lv_output.

*  Call the method by passing the values to the Second class
   CALL METHOD lo_class2->INTERFACE1~DISPLAY
               EXPORTING im_invno   = p_inv_no
               IMPORTING ex_output = lv_output1.
*  Output
   WRITE : /'The first  output is',lv_output.
   WRITE : /'The second output is',lv_output1.

 Analysis:

In the above, the same method display (of the Same Interface) has got two different Implementations in two different classes.

We have also have multiple Interfaces inside a Class.

Inheritance:

  • Creating SubClass from Parent Class is called Inheritance.  The concept of Inheritance allows us to derive new classes from Existing classes.
  • Multiple Inheritance is not possible directly in sap abap but it can be achieved via Interfaces.

In Local class in se38 the syntax is:

  • CLASS Classname_Par DEFINITION FINAL.
  • CLASS Classname_Ch DEFINITION INHERITING FROM Classname_Par.
  • If the class is marked as Final then we cannot Inherit further.
  • In se24, we can check the checkbox FINAL.
  • In Local class in se38, we can add FINAL to the Class Definition.

In Local class in se38 the syntax for FINAL is:

CLASS Classname DEFINITION FINAL.

ABSTRACT Class:

  • Abstract Class is a type of class that has atleast one Abstract Method. We can have more than one
  • Abstract method is the method that has only Definition and will not have any implementation.
  • We cannot create any Object of Abstract Class. We can create Objects for Subclasses of Abstract class.
  • Never mark Abstract Class as FINAL as we need to create Subclass.

TCode SE24:

Create the method:

Make the Method as Abstract:

When you click on Source Code we will get a message as below and we cannot write code inside the Abstract method:

Now we will create the subclass:

Change the SuperClass to the Abstract Class Created:

Redefine the method and write the logic in the method:

Now let us create Subclass2.

Method Overriding:

  • In the above the same method in the abstract class has got two different behaviours in the two Subclasses.
  • We Overrided the Abstract Class Method in Subclass 1 and Overrided the same method in Subclass2.
  • This is called as Method Overriding and it exists between Super Class and Sub Class.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZPROG_ABSTRACT_SE24
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZPROG_ABSTRACT_SE24.
* Data Declaration
  DATA : lo_ch_obj1  TYPE REF TO ZCL_ABSTRACT_CHILD,
         lo_ch_obj2  TYPE REF TO ZCL_ABSTRACT_CHILD_1.
* Create the Object for First Class
  CREATE OBJECT lo_ch_obj1.
* Create the Object for Second Class
  CREATE OBJECT lo_ch_obj2.
* Calling the method of the first Class
  CALL METHOD lo_ch_obj1->display.
* Calling the method of the Second Class
  CALL METHOD lo_ch_obj2->display.

ABSTRACT CLASS in Program:

Output:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZPROG_ABSTRACT_LOCAL
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZPROG_ABSTRACT_LOCAL.
* Abstract Class Definition
  CLASS CLASS_ABSTRACT DEFINITION ABSTRACT.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
       METHODS DISPLAY ABSTRACT.
  ENDCLASS.
* First Child Class Definition
  CLASS CLASS_ABS_CHILD1 DEFINITION INHERITING FROM CLASS_ABSTRACT.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
       METHODS DISPLAY REDEFINITION.
  ENDCLASS.

* Second Child Class Definition
  CLASS CLASS_ABS_CHILD2 DEFINITION INHERITING FROM CLASS_ABSTRACT.
    PUBLIC SECTION.
       METHODS DISPLAY REDEFINITION.
  ENDCLASS.
* First Child Class Implementation
  CLASS CLASS_ABS_CHILD1 IMPLEMENTATION.
     METHOD DISPLAY.
        WRITE : / 'This is Abstract method redefined in the FIRST Child Class'.
     ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.
* Second Child Class Implementation
  CLASS CLASS_ABS_CHILD2 IMPLEMENTATION.
     METHOD DISPLAY.
        WRITE : / 'This is Abstract method redefined in the SECOND Child Class'.
     ENDMETHOD.
  ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
* Data Declaration
  DATA : lo_object1 TYPE REF TO CLASS_ABS_CHILD1,
         lo_object2 TYPE REF TO CLASS_ABS_CHILD2.
* Creating the Object
  CREATE OBJECT lo_object1.
  CREATE OBJECT lo_object2.
* Calling the Method
  CALL METHOD lo_object1->DISPLAY.
  CALL METHOD lo_object2->DISPLAY.

Difference between Abstract Class and Interface:

Abstract Class:

  • Abstract Method can be either Public / Protected. An Abstract Method cannot be Private as it has to be Redefined In child class.
  • In Abstract class one method is Abstract. Other methods can be Abstract (or) Non-Abstract.
  • We need to redefine all the Abstract method in the child Class.
  • Multiple Inheritance is NOT Possible.
  • A Static Method Inside an Abstract class cannot be marked as Abstract method as Static method cannot be redefined in Child Class.

Interface:

  • All Interface Methods are ONLY Public.
  • Interface methods may / may not be redefined in Child Class.
  • Multiple Inheritance is Possible in Interfaces.
  • In the Class Using the Interface, we can write code into the Interface Method by double clicking the Interface method.

SINGLETON CLASS:

  • Singleton class in basically Single Object (or) Instance creation.
  • We use Singleton class when we want to have only one Instance of the Class.
  • Singleton Class is a Class that has only one Object / Instance creation.
  • We cannot create object or Instance of Singleton Class outside the Class and we write the logic for the Instance creation inside the class itself.

Steps to Create Singleton Class:

  • We declare the Instance of the Class as Private and this prevents External Instance / Object generation of the Class.
  • Also, we declare a Static Variable within the class to hold the Single Instance and is usually TYPE REF to Class itself. We are declaring Static Variable as the we need to access from the Static Method created in next step (we cannot access Instance variable from Static method).
  • Create a Static Method to return the Single Instance of the Object created. As a Best Practice, it is suggested that we provide the Method name as GET_INSTANCE. The reason we are creating Static Method ( instead of Instance Method ) is that the Object must be created within the class and we will not be able to call Instance method from outside the class without the Object being created from outside the class. We add the Returning (or) Exporting Parameter to this Method.
  • Agent Class (in Persistence Class) is Best Example of Singleton Class as all database operations go through a Single Instance.

TCode: SE24

Output:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  ZPROG_SINGLETON_1
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT  ZPROG_SINGLETON_1.
* Data Declaration
  DATA : LO_OBJECT TYPE REF TO ZCL_SINGLETON_2.
*  CREATE OBJECT LO_OBJECT. " This is not allowed as we cannot create 
*  Singleton Object Outside the Class.
* Calling the Method to get the Object Instance
  CALL METHOD zcl_singleton_2=>get_instance
    IMPORTING
      RO_OBJECT = LO_OBJECT.
* Calling the Instance method using the object reference obtained in previous * step
  CALL METHOD lo_OBJECT->display
    .

SINGLETON CLASS in PROGRAM:

Output:

Persistent Class:

I have published a blog on Persistent Class in SAP OO ABAP. The link for this is given below for your ready reference:

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-members/persistent-class-in-sap-oo-abap/ba-p/14402606

Exception Class:

I have published a blog on Exception Class in SAP OO ABAP. The link for this is given below for your ready reference:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/exception-class-in-sap-oo-abap/ba-p/14402695 

Unit Test Class:

I have published a blog on Unit Test Class in SAP OO ABAP. The link for this is given below for your ready reference:

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-members/unit-test-class-in-sap-oo-abap/ba-p/14402743 

This blog explains various concepts of SAP OO ABAP. Also, I have given references to other blogs published by me to explain Practical scenarios where I have used.

                                                                          * End of Document *

entry.title: Sapphire 2026 recap: Joule for Developers Agentic ABAP AI
entry.content:
TL;DR: What you learned on Sapphire 2026 is that in general SAP is evolving from standalone AI tools to an orchestration layer that automates multi-step workflows for business user and developers.
The ABAP platform team is very proud to be on the forefront of this and this was the major focus for all Sapphire ABAP AI sessions. 
In a nutshell we have announced the following key benefits for customers and partners of what will be made generally available in the first weeks of June:

 

  1. ABAP AI now supports Agentic orchestration in Eclipse and VS Code with out-of-the-box ABAP MCP Server lots of great MCP tools.
  2. ABAP AI is available for lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition >=2021.
  3. Simplified enablement of ABAP AI via SAP for Me and Central Business Configuration with a new commercial model based on AI Unit consumption (->details how to purchase and on-board will follow soon and will be linked here).
  4. Faster innovation with Side-by-Side consumption of SAP and 3rd party Agentic IDE, Orchestrators and LLMs (e.g. Microsoft, Amazon, IBM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral…)
  5. Agentic Custom Code Migration accelerates ECC to S/4HANA Transformation -> see the demo below

 

Here is a demo of the first iteration of the agentic custom code migration with mass ready ATC AI quick fixes using Amazon Q in Eclipse (on Sapphire we showed also GitHub CoPilot as orchestrator):

If you want to understand deeper the context of the demo have a look into my Sapphire recap. Here I explain in more detail what it means for you as customer and developer and I also give a bit of outlook to what comes next:

 

More details can be found here (stay tuned for a how-to-purchase agentic ABAP AI with AI units):

  1. Sapphire 2026: GA announcement for agentic ABAP AI (Sonja Lienard)
  2. Sapphire 2026: ABAP AI - Chapter 3: We go agentic! (Alexander Rother)
  3. FAQ document for frequent questions
  4. Public Roadmap: SAP Help / SAP Roadmap Explorer
  5. Help & Guides
    1. Free Promo only (not yet AI Units): Step-by-step guide how to activate free promo for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
    2. Free Promo only (not yet AI Units): Joule for Developers (J4D) for S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup Guide
    3. SAP ABAP AI Discovery Center
    4. SAP BTP Discovery Center (Free Tier Services)
    5. Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
    6. YouTube playlist for Joule for Developers (J4D)
    7. Joule for Developers Help (J4D)
    8. Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud and SAP Joule for developers (RAP120) 
    9. ADT Eclipse AI Capabilities Help
    10. ABAP AI SDK Help
    11. ABAP-1 Help

More high-level insights about Joule for Developers/ABAP AI and ABAP platform can be found here . Here you find more on ABAP- 1,

entry.title: Picking List
entry.content:
I have analysed the requirement to sort the data in the Picking List form (CO02 output) based on Storage Bin in ascending order.

Technically no feasibility within the current Public Cloud framework.

 

From a technical perspective in S/4HANA Public Cloud, we are facing the following limitations:

  • The current data source FDP_PP_MOR_PICK_LIST_SRV is a standard Form Data Provider, and modifications are not permitted.
  • Sorting at the Adobe form level is not feasible, as the form is based on XML data, and the sequence of records is controlled by the backend.
  • Attempts to implement sorting using JavaScript in the form were not successful due to Public Cloud restrictions on client-side scripting.
  • Sorting logic implemented through Custom Business Logic (BAdI) does not impact the form output, as it does not control the sequence of data passed to the Adobe form.

Based on the above analysis, applying storage bin–wise sorting for this form is not technically feasible within the current Public Cloud framework</

entry.title: Sapphire 2026 Key Take Aways for Data Leaders
entry.content:
Here are some of the take-aways from SAP Sapphire Madrid in May 2026, for us data professionals who are keen to learn more about how SAP is elevating data to its strategic pinnacle.  

We’ve always known the importance of data so it was rewarding to hear the same impressions from the SAP leadership.  The Business Data Cloud (BDC) featured prominently in the keynotes, showcasing its evolution into a robust, integrated business data fabric that enables the business value with contextual SAP and non-SAP data. . Announcements highlighted significant advancements in BDC’s semantic layer, promising unprecedented data quality and harmonization across diverse data sources – a critical enabler for reliable analytics and AI. We saw concrete examples of BDC streamlining data management and enhancing data governance, underpinning compliance and trusted decision-making, which is paramount for data leaders. During my Data Products presentation I highlighted the soon to be generally available Data Product Studio, a BDC toolset that expands data product search, enables the creation of custom data products and data product management.  An exciting development is the use of AI agents to automate many of the data product management tasks, starting with data product creation.

Strategic partnerships were a major focal point at the Sapphire Madrid event.. SAP revealed expanded collaborations with leading hyperscalers, ensuring seamless, scalable data ingestion and secure infrastructure for advanced generative AI model training. Furthermore, new alliances with specialized AI firms were announced, designed to embed sophisticated, industry-specific AI capabilities directly into SAP applications, augmenting offerings like Joule. This emphasis on unified, governed data through BDC, coupled with a robust partner ecosystem for AI innovation, marks an exciting, data-driven future for the intelligent enterprise, grounded in trust and accessibility.  As SAP expands its hyperscaler collaboration model it’s great to see announcements that truly underscored SAP’s commitment to flexibility, scale and leveraging best of breed infrastructure for its data and AI strategy.  It wasn’t just about presence but deeper, more integrated capabilities with Google Cloud Platform, Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure.

 The announcement of the Dremio and Reltio acquisitions, finalized shortly before Sapphire, brought cutting-edge data virtualization and semantic modeling platforms into the SAP fold. For a global data executive, this is crucial because it promises to accelerate the realization of a true business data fabric. It means we can logically integrate data from disparate sources – SAP and non-SAP, on-premise and cloud – without costly physical replication. These acquisitions will give our customers a governed, context-rich foundation for agentic AI, analytics, and business applications. Specifically, these new capabilities, aligned with the Business Data Cloud will significantly improve data lineage, ensure consistent data definitions across the enterprise, and provide a unified, trusted view of information critical for advanced analytics and AI, directly enhancing BDC’s capabilities and easing compliance burdens.

 So, it was certainly refreshing to see data and AI take center stage at Sapphire and I’m confident that over the next quarters/before the end of 2026 we will see even more capabilities that enable SAP’s data and AI strategy: the release of new assistants and agents, more strategic partnerships and further evidence of SAP’s open data source approach.

entry.title: Re: Cash Journal
entry.content:
Hi Sagar,

I understand that your customer is looking for a consolidated view of their cash transactions. However, to give it to you straight: based on the current SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system design and documentation, there is no standard out-of-the-box "Consolidated Cash Journal Report" app.

The two standard apps you mentioned - Cash Journal and Post Cash Journal Entries, are strictly operational tools. They are built for day-to-day, single-screen transaction processing at the individual cash journal level, which is why there are no cross-cash-journal or cross-branch consolidation filters natively built into them.

To provide you with the most accurate workaround, I must first suggest a clarifying question to ask your customer or technical team:

How is the organizational concept of a "branch" technically mapped in your customer's Public Cloud system? > (For example, is it configured as a Profit Center, a Segment, or even a company code?)

Assuming "branch" is mapped to a Profit Center (which is the standard best practice in S/4HANA Cloud), you can completely fulfill this requirement by shifting your reporting focus from the Cash Journal subledger to the General Ledger. Since all cash transactions post directly to the Universal Journal, you have a few alternative solutions:

1. App: Display Line Items in General Ledger

Instead of looking at the subledger, use the standard G/L reporting apps to consolidate the data.

  • Method: Open the app and filter by the specific G/L accounts or journal Entry Type assigned to your Cash Journals.

  • Consolidation: Adjust the layout to include the "Profit Center" (your branch) in the columns. You can group and subtotal by this dimension to see a consolidated view of receipts, payments, and balances per branch.

2. App: Journal Entry Analyzer (F0956A)

If the customer prefers a multidimensional, pivot-table style report.

  • Method: Filter the dataset by your Petty Cash G/L accounts or Journal Entry Type.

  • Consolidation: Drag and drop the "Profit Center" dimension into the rows, and the monetary amounts into the columns. This instantly creates a consolidated summary of cash movements across all branches.

3. App: Custom Analytical Queries

If the business strictly requires a report that visually mimics a traditional cash journal (Opening Balance, Inflows, Outflows, Closing Balance), but consolidated by branch.

  • Method: Build a custom query using the Custom Analytical Queries app based on a standard Universal Journal CDS view. Hardcode a filter for the Cash Journal G/L accounts or Journal Entry Type and expose the branch dimension as a mandatory row or prompt.

Official SAP Documentation for Reference:

  • Cash Journals Overview
  • Display Line Items in General Ledger
  • Journal Entry Analyser - New app

Once you verify exactly how the branch is mapped in the system environment, these alternative paths will provide the consolidated reporting your customer needs.

Best Regards,

Michal
